text: The Americans have need of the telephone, but we do not. We have plenty of messenger boys.
author: Sir William Preece

didItHoldUp.analysis: Britain's Chief Engineer of the Post Office essentially argued that running children through the streets was a superior communication system to electrical voice transmission. Britain adopted the telephone within years, and Preece himself later became a champion of wireless telegraphy. The quote captures a timeless pattern: incumbents in communication systems consistently underestimate the next technology, because they can't imagine why anyone would want something they've never experienced.
